Course Title: Who Owns AI Decisions in the Field?
Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Field operators, agricultural workers, mining technicians, energy plant operators, forestry workers, site supervisors, operations managers, field engineers, plant managers, resource extraction supervisors
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Field Operations, Production, Engineering, Agriculture, Mining, Energy, Natural Resources, Site Management
Description: Accountability across operators, engineers, and leadership.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with day-to-day operations in at least one agricultural, forestry, fishing, mining, or energy setting is helpful, though no prior knowledge of artificial intelligence or data systems is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key roles responsible for AI-driven decisions across agricultural, forestry, fishing, mining, and energy operations, and explain how accountability is shared between operators, engineers, and leadership. You will also apply practical frameworks to recognise when and how to escalate concerns, ensuring that responsibility for AI decisions is clearly understood at every level of your organisation.
